item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice skip. I really enjoy rivals to the Foundation's cause, and enjoy the three proposals on how to handle the site at the end. I am glad that the conversion is somewhat gradual, particularly Gabriel's log.

To add a little mystery to the SCP, maybe have something that hints that this is the original Foundation site, and that the current Foundation is the pretender. Not that I think this should be true, but throwing the question out there would be interesting I think. I mean with all the anomalies and history rewrites, why do we believe so strongly that the Foundation is exactly what it claims to be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Done" could perhaps be "performed" or "carried out."

Good point, I'll change that. Thanks for upboat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Nitpick:

After initial discovery, all progress in exploration and extraction of SCP-4550-A has been done by remote controlled drones.

"Done" could perhaps be "performed" or "carried out."

That's it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ! The formatting is absolutely lovely. Liked the login part- honestly, there are a lot of SCPs that use the 'Warning! do not look!' label, but a login format fits the narrative better. Love the concept of an anomalous facility..!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1930 with some SCP-2140 thrown in there. Interesting, but it feels unfulfilling to read and it's not forbodingly creepy enough like 1930 and other similar anomalous Foundation sites.

Edit: Just wanna add that I think this should be Euclid. It's contained for the most part and it's not actively causing any sort of disruption to anyone or anything surrounding it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very minor thing but the "o" in your article's title: "operational" is lowercase, and my OCD is kicking in. Feel free to leave it be or not, it's just making me a little twitchy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 man. I've always liked the concept of off-the-books or otherwise anomalous Foundation facilities. It's short and sweet and portrays exactly what it needs to

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much! Are there particular sentences that are odd?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good; I like the slow burn of Gabriel losing his mind, the little hints at deeper lore, and the overall premise. I think it has the potential to be great, if there were a bit more to it. You hint at what this place is, involving time manipulating objects, but I would really enjoy something a little more substantive in that direction. Not necessarily a full explanation; just a little bit more. Then again, I haven't read the linked tales yet. Maybe they'll provide more information.

(also, you missed an "SCP-XXXX")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Phrasing and sentences are a bit odd in places and you missed at least one XXXX, but overall I really like this.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to Eskobar, Scorpion451, Zyn Zachary Maxwell for the critique and addition of the blackbox addendum (as well as letting me use one of his characters), taylor_itkin and Veiedhimaedhr. Thanks to LadyKatie for giving me the final kick to post it.

I wrote this SCP so slowly that I'm positive that I didn't get everyone that critiqued it. If you're one of those people, let me know here or shoot me a PM about it and I'll add you.

Image credits:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_power_plant#/media/File:Kewaunee.jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=596

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Aerial_photographs_of_prisons#/media/File:FCI_Phoenix_USGS.jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Night_vis\261
